created.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to repository rulesets. For more information about repository rulesets, see "Managing rulesets." For more information on managing rulesets via the APIs, see Repository ruleset in the GraphQL documentation or "Repository rules" and "Organization rules in the REST API documentation."

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Administration" repository or organization permission.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: repository
bodyParameters.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Webhook payloads contain the repository property when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

bodyParameters.name: repository_ruleset
bodyParameters.description: A set of rules to apply when specified conditions are met.

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: The ID of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: target
childParamsGroups.description: The target of the ruleset.

childParamsGroups.name: source_type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of the source of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: source
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the source

childParamsGroups.name: enforcement
childParamsGroups.description: The enforcement level of the ruleset. evaluate allows admins to test rules before enforcing them. Admins can view insights on the Rule Insights page. evaluate is not available for the repository target.

childParamsGroups.name: bypass_actors
childParamsGroups.description: The actors that can bypass the rules in this ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: actor_id
childParamsGroups.description: The ID of the actor that can bypass a ruleset. Required for Integration, RepositoryRole, and Team actor types. If actor_type is OrganizationAdmin, actor_id is ignored. If actor_type is DeployKey, this should be null. If actor_type is EnterpriseOwner, actor_id is ignored. OrganizationAdmin, EnterpriseOwner and EnterpriseRole are not applicable for personal repositories.

childParamsGroups.name: actor_type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of actor that can bypass a ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: bypass_mode
childParamsGroups.description: When the specified actor can bypass the ruleset. pull_request means that an actor can only bypass rules on pull requests. pull_request is not applicable for the DeployKey actor type. Also, pull_request is only applicable to branch rulesets. When bypass_mode is exempt, rules will not be run for that actor and a bypass audit entry will not be created.

childParamsGroups.name: current_user_can_bypass
childParamsGroups.description: The bypass type of the user making the API request for this ruleset. This field is only returned when querying the repository-level endpoint.

childParamsGroups.name: href
childParamsGroups.description: The URL of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: href
childParamsGroups.description: The html URL of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: creation
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permission to create matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: update
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permission to update matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: update_allows_fetch_and_merge
childParamsGroups.description: Branch can pull changes from its upstream repository

childParamsGroups.name: deletion
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permissions to delete matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: required_linear_history
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent merge commits from being pushed to matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: merge_queue
childParamsGroups.description: Merges must be performed via a merge queue.

childParamsGroups.name: check_response_timeout_minutes
childParamsGroups.description: Maximum time for a required status check to report a conclusion. After this much time has elapsed, checks that have not reported a conclusion will be assumed to have failed

childParamsGroups.name: grouping_strategy
childParamsGroups.description: When set to ALLGREEN, the merge commit created by merge queue for each PR in the group must pass all required checks to merge. When set to HEADGREEN, only the commit at the head of the merge group, i.e. the commit containing changes from all of the PRs in the group, must pass its required checks to merge.

childParamsGroups.name: max_entries_to_build
childParamsGroups.description: Limit the number of queued pull requests requesting checks and workflow runs at the same time.

childParamsGroups.name: max_entries_to_merge
childParamsGroups.description: The maximum number of PRs that will be merged together in a group.

childParamsGroups.name: merge_method
childParamsGroups.description: Method to use when merging changes from queued pull requests.

childParamsGroups.name: min_entries_to_merge
childParamsGroups.description: The minimum number of PRs that will be merged together in a group.

childParamsGroups.name: min_entries_to_merge_wait_minutes
childParamsGroups.description: The time merge queue should wait after the first PR is added to the queue for the minimum group size to be met. After this time has elapsed, the minimum group size will be ignored and a smaller group will be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_deployments
childParamsGroups.description: Choose which environments must be successfully deployed to before refs can be pushed into a ref that matches this rule.

childParamsGroups.name: required_deployment_environments
childParamsGroups.description: The environments that must be successfully deployed to before branches can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_signatures
childParamsGroups.description: Commits pushed to matching refs must have verified signatures.

childParamsGroups.name: pull_request
childParamsGroups.description: Require all commits be made to a non-target branch and submitted via a pull request before they can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: allowed_merge_methods
childParamsGroups.description: Array of allowed merge methods. Allowed values include merge, squash, and rebase. At least one option must be enabled. Supported values are: merge, squash, rebase

childParamsGroups.name: dismiss_stale_reviews_on_push
childParamsGroups.description: New, reviewable commits pushed will dismiss previous pull request review approvals.

childParamsGroups.name: require_code_owner_review
childParamsGroups.description: Require an approving review in pull requests that modify files that have a designated code owner.

childParamsGroups.name: require_last_push_approval
childParamsGroups.description: Whether the most recent reviewable push must be approved by someone other than the person who pushed it.

childParamsGroups.name: required_approving_review_count
childParamsGroups.description: The number of approving reviews that are required before a pull request can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_review_thread_resolution
childParamsGroups.description: All conversations on code must be resolved before a pull request can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_reviewers
childParamsGroups.description:
Note

required_reviewers is in beta and subject to change.

A collection of reviewers and associated file patterns. Each reviewer has a list of file patterns which determine the files that reviewer is required to review.

childParamsGroups.name: file_patterns
childParamsGroups.description: Array of file patterns. Pull requests which change matching files must be approved by the specified team. File patterns use fnmatch syntax.

childParamsGroups.name: minimum_approvals
childParamsGroups.description: Minimum number of approvals required from the specified team. If set to zero, the team will be added to the pull request but approval is optional.

childParamsGroups.name: reviewer
childParamsGroups.description: A required reviewing team

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: ID of the reviewer which must review changes to matching files.

childParamsGroups.name: type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of the reviewer

childParamsGroups.name: required_status_checks
childParamsGroups.description: Choose which status checks must pass before the ref is updated. When enabled, commits must first be pushed to another ref where the checks pass.

childParamsGroups.name: do_not_enforce_on_create
childParamsGroups.description: Allow repositories and branches to be created if a check would otherwise prohibit it.

childParamsGroups.name: required_status_checks
childParamsGroups.description: Status checks that are required.

childParamsGroups.name: context
childParamsGroups.description: The status check context name that must be present on the commit.

childParamsGroups.name: integration_id
childParamsGroups.description: The optional integration ID that this status check must originate from.

childParamsGroups.name: strict_required_status_checks_policy
childParamsGroups.description: Whether pull requests targeting a matching branch must be tested with the latest code. This setting will not take effect unless at least one status check is enabled.

childParamsGroups.name: non_fast_forward
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent users with push access from force pushing to refs.

childParamsGroups.name: commit_message_pattern
childParamsGroups.description: Parameters to be used for the commit_message_pattern rule

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: How this rule appears when configuring it.

childParamsGroups.name: negate
childParamsGroups.description: If true, the rule will fail if the pattern matches.

childParamsGroups.name: operator
childParamsGroups.description: The operator to use for matching.

childParamsGroups.name: pattern
childParamsGroups.description: The pattern to match with.

childParamsGroups.name: commit_author_email_pattern
childParamsGroups.description: Parameters to be used for the commit_author_email_pattern rule

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: How this rule appears when configuring it.

childParamsGroups.name: negate
childParamsGroups.description: If true, the rule will fail if the pattern matches.

childParamsGroups.name: operator
childParamsGroups.description: The operator to use for matching.

childParamsGroups.name: pattern
childParamsGroups.description: The pattern to match with.

childParamsGroups.name: committer_email_pattern
childParamsGroups.description: Parameters to be used for the committer_email_pattern rule

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: How this rule appears when configuring it.

childParamsGroups.name: negate
childParamsGroups.description: If true, the rule will fail if the pattern matches.

childParamsGroups.name: operator
childParamsGroups.description: The operator to use for matching.

childParamsGroups.name: pattern
childParamsGroups.description: The pattern to match with.

childParamsGroups.name: branch_name_pattern
childParamsGroups.description: Parameters to be used for the branch_name_pattern rule

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: How this rule appears when configuring it.

childParamsGroups.name: negate
childParamsGroups.description: If true, the rule will fail if the pattern matches.

childParamsGroups.name: operator
childParamsGroups.description: The operator to use for matching.

childParamsGroups.name: pattern
childParamsGroups.description: The pattern to match with.

childParamsGroups.name: tag_name_pattern
childParamsGroups.description: Parameters to be used for the tag_name_pattern rule

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: How this rule appears when configuring it.

childParamsGroups.name: negate
childParamsGroups.description: If true, the rule will fail if the pattern matches.

childParamsGroups.name: operator
childParamsGroups.description: The operator to use for matching.

childParamsGroups.name: pattern
childParamsGroups.description: The pattern to match with.

childParamsGroups.name: workflows
childParamsGroups.description: Require all changes made to a targeted branch to pass the specified workflows before they can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: do_not_enforce_on_create
childParamsGroups.description: Allow repositories and branches to be created if a check would otherwise prohibit it.

childParamsGroups.name: workflows
childParamsGroups.description: Workflows that must pass for this rule to pass.

childParamsGroups.name: path
childParamsGroups.description: The path to the workflow file

childParamsGroups.name: ref
childParamsGroups.description: The ref (branch or tag) of the workflow file to use

childParamsGroups.name: repository_id
childParamsGroups.description: The ID of the repository where the workflow is defined

childParamsGroups.name: sha
childParamsGroups.description: The commit SHA of the workflow file to use

childParamsGroups.name: code_scanning
childParamsGroups.description: Choose which tools must provide code scanning results before the reference is updated. When configured, code scanning must be enabled and have results for both the commit and the reference being updated.

childParamsGroups.name: code_scanning_tools
childParamsGroups.description: Tools that must provide code scanning results for this rule to pass.

childParamsGroups.name: alerts_threshold
childParamsGroups.description: The severity level at which code scanning results that raise alerts block a reference update. For more information on alert severity levels, see "About code scanning alerts."

childParamsGroups.name: security_alerts_threshold
childParamsGroups.description: The severity level at which code scanning results that raise security alerts block a reference update. For more information on security severity levels, see "About code scanning alerts."

childParamsGroups.name: tool
childParamsGroups.description: The name of a code scanning tool

childParamsGroups.name: copilot_code_review
childParamsGroups.description: Request Copilot code review for new pull requests automatically if the author has access to Copilot code review and their premium requests quota has not reached the limit.

childParamsGroups.name: review_draft_pull_requests
childParamsGroups.description: Copilot automatically reviews draft pull requests before they are marked as ready for review.

childParamsGroups.name: review_on_push
childParamsGroups.description: Copilot automatically reviews each new push to the pull request.

childParamsGroups.name: file_path_restriction
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent commits that include changes in specified file and folder paths from being pushed to the commit graph. This includes absolute paths that contain file names.

childParamsGroups.name: restricted_file_paths
childParamsGroups.description: The file paths that are restricted from being pushed to the commit graph.

childParamsGroups.name: max_file_path_length
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent commits that include file paths that exceed the specified character limit from being pushed to the commit graph.

childParamsGroups.name: max_file_path_length
childParamsGroups.description: The maximum amount of characters allowed in file paths.

childParamsGroups.name: file_extension_restriction
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent commits that include files with specified file extensions from being pushed to the commit graph.

childParamsGroups.name: restricted_file_extensions
childParamsGroups.description: The file extensions that are restricted from being pushed to the commit graph.

childParamsGroups.name: max_file_size
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent commits with individual files that exceed the specified limit from being pushed to the commit graph.

childParamsGroups.name: max_file_size
childParamsGroups.description: The maximum file size allowed in megabytes. This limit does not apply to Git Large File Storage (Git LFS).

childParamsGroups.description: The ID of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: target
childParamsGroups.description: The target of the ruleset.

childParamsGroups.name: source_type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of the source of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: source
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the source

childParamsGroups.name: enforcement
childParamsGroups.description: The enforcement level of the ruleset. evaluate allows admins to test rules before enforcing them. Admins can view insights on the Rule Insights page. evaluate is not available for the repository target.

childParamsGroups.name: bypass_actors
childParamsGroups.description: The actors that can bypass the rules in this ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: actor_id
childParamsGroups.description: The ID of the actor that can bypass a ruleset. Required for Integration, RepositoryRole, and Team actor types. If actor_type is OrganizationAdmin, actor_id is ignored. If actor_type is DeployKey, this should be null. If actor_type is EnterpriseOwner, actor_id is ignored. OrganizationAdmin, EnterpriseOwner and EnterpriseRole are not applicable for personal repositories.

childParamsGroups.name: actor_type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of actor that can bypass a ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: bypass_mode
childParamsGroups.description: When the specified actor can bypass the ruleset. pull_request means that an actor can only bypass rules on pull requests. pull_request is not applicable for the DeployKey actor type. Also, pull_request is only applicable to branch rulesets. When bypass_mode is exempt, rules will not be run for that actor and a bypass audit entry will not be created.

childParamsGroups.name: current_user_can_bypass
childParamsGroups.description: The bypass type of the user making the API request for this ruleset. This field is only returned when querying the repository-level endpoint.

childParamsGroups.name: href
childParamsGroups.description: The URL of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: href
childParamsGroups.description: The html URL of the ruleset

childParamsGroups.name: creation
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permission to create matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: update
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permission to update matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: update_allows_fetch_and_merge
childParamsGroups.description: Branch can pull changes from its upstream repository

childParamsGroups.name: deletion
childParamsGroups.description: Only allow users with bypass permissions to delete matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: required_linear_history
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent merge commits from being pushed to matching refs.

childParamsGroups.name: merge_queue
childParamsGroups.description: Merges must be performed via a merge queue.

childParamsGroups.name: check_response_timeout_minutes
childParamsGroups.description: Maximum time for a required status check to report a conclusion. After this much time has elapsed, checks that have not reported a conclusion will be assumed to have failed

childParamsGroups.name: grouping_strategy
childParamsGroups.description: When set to ALLGREEN, the merge commit created by merge queue for each PR in the group must pass all required checks to merge. When set to HEADGREEN, only the commit at the head of the merge group, i.e. the commit containing changes from all of the PRs in the group, must pass its required checks to merge.

childParamsGroups.name: max_entries_to_build
childParamsGroups.description: Limit the number of queued pull requests requesting checks and workflow runs at the same time.

childParamsGroups.name: max_entries_to_merge
childParamsGroups.description: The maximum number of PRs that will be merged together in a group.

childParamsGroups.name: merge_method
childParamsGroups.description: Method to use when merging changes from queued pull requests.

childParamsGroups.name: min_entries_to_merge
childParamsGroups.description: The minimum number of PRs that will be merged together in a group.

childParamsGroups.name: min_entries_to_merge_wait_minutes
childParamsGroups.description: The time merge queue should wait after the first PR is added to the queue for the minimum group size to be met. After this time has elapsed, the minimum group size will be ignored and a smaller group will be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_deployments
childParamsGroups.description: Choose which environments must be successfully deployed to before refs can be pushed into a ref that matches this rule.

childParamsGroups.name: required_deployment_environments
childParamsGroups.description: The environments that must be successfully deployed to before branches can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_signatures
childParamsGroups.description: Commits pushed to matching refs must have verified signatures.

childParamsGroups.name: pull_request
childParamsGroups.description: Require all commits be made to a non-target branch and submitted via a pull request before they can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: allowed_merge_methods
childParamsGroups.description: Array of allowed merge methods. Allowed values include merge, squash, and rebase. At least one option must be enabled. Supported values are: merge, squash, rebase

childParamsGroups.name: dismiss_stale_reviews_on_push
childParamsGroups.description: New, reviewable commits pushed will dismiss previous pull request review approvals.

childParamsGroups.name: require_code_owner_review
childParamsGroups.description: Require an approving review in pull requests that modify files that have a designated code owner.

childParamsGroups.name: require_last_push_approval
childParamsGroups.description: Whether the most recent reviewable push must be approved by someone other than the person who pushed it.

childParamsGroups.name: required_approving_review_count
childParamsGroups.description: The number of approving reviews that are required before a pull request can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_review_thread_resolution
childParamsGroups.description: All conversations on code must be resolved before a pull request can be merged.

childParamsGroups.name: required_reviewers
childParamsGroups.description:
Note

required_reviewers is in beta and subject to change.

A collection of reviewers and associated file patterns. Each reviewer has a list of file patterns which determine the files that reviewer is required to review.

childParamsGroups.name: file_patterns
childParamsGroups.description: Array of file patterns. Pull requests which change matching files must be approved by the specified team. File patterns use fnmatch syntax.

childParamsGroups.name: minimum_approvals
childParamsGroups.description: Minimum number of approvals required from the specified team. If set to zero, the team will be added to the pull request but approval is optional.

childParamsGroups.name: reviewer
childParamsGroups.description: A required reviewing team

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: ID of the reviewer which must review changes to matching files.

childParamsGroups.name: type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of the reviewer

childParamsGroups.name: required_status_checks
childParamsGroups.description: Choose which status checks must pass before the ref is updated. When enabled, commits must first be pushed to another ref where the checks pass.

childParamsGroups.name: do_not_enforce_on_create
childParamsGroups.description: Allow repositories and branches to be created if a check would otherwise prohibit it.

childParamsGroups.name: required_status_checks
childParamsGroups.description: Status checks that are required.

childParamsGroups.name: context
childParamsGroups.description: The status check context name that must be present on the commit.

childParamsGroups.name: integration_id
childParamsGroups.description: The optional integration ID that this status check must originate from.

childParamsGroups.name: strict_required_status_checks_policy
childParamsGroups.description: Whether pull requests targeting a matching branch must be tested with the latest code. This setting will not take effect unless at least one status check is enabled.

childParamsGroups.name: non_fast_forward
childParamsGroups.description: Prevent users with push access from force pushing to refs.

childParamsGroups.description: When set to ALLGREEN, the merge commit created by merge queue for each PR in the group must pass all required checks to merge. childParamsGroups.name: min_entries_to_merge_wait_minutes
